sourceMovie.title: The Babysitter
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Babysitter works as a comedy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The comedy and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ready or Not
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Babysitter clicked for you, Ready or Not (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Death Becomes Her

faq.question: What should I watch after The Babysitter?
faq.answer: Try Ready or Not, Death Becomes Her, A Haunted House 2—each shares DNA with The Babysitter while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Babysitter?
faq.answer: The Babysitter (2017) is best described as Comedy, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Babysitter part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Babysitter Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
